title: bloomeria crocea var. montana
intro: Bloomeria crocea var. montana, also known as the yellow sand verbena, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the asphodel family. It is native to the southwestern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Bloomeria crocea var. montana typically include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical and internal use.
